title: dubautia lonchophylla
intro: Dubautia lonchophylla, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Hawaii. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its unique chemical composition. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and fever, and has also been used to promote skin health and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
